etymology.description: 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") + phonetic 悤 (cōng) -> listening ear leading to intelligence
etymology.interpretation: The character 聰(cōng, "clever") represents the concept of intelligence through sensitivity to sound and willingness to listen. The semantic component 耳(耳) (ěr, "ear") signifies listening, while the phonetic component 悤 (cōng) helps convey the sound. Together, they depict someone who is keen at understanding and learning via hearing, reflecting cleverness.

examples.chinese: 他很聪明(他很聰明)，总是能找到解决问题的方法(總是能找到解決問題的方法)。
examples.pinyin: Tā hěn cōngmíng, zǒng shì néng zhǎodào jiějué wèntí de fāngfǎ.
examples.english: He is very clever and always finds ways to solve problems.

examples.chinese: 聰明的人(聰明的人)通常很有創意(通常很有創意)。
examples.pinyin: Cōngmíng de rén tōngcháng hěn yǒu chuàngyì.
examples.english: Clever people are often very creative.

examples.chinese: 这本书适合聪明的学生(這本書適合聰明的學生)阅读(閱讀)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū shìhé cōngmíng de xuéshēng yuèdú.
examples.english: This book is suitable for clever students to read.
